texto: There isn't a better feeling in the world—not an orgasm, not a first kiss, not even that glorious soaring sensation you get when those first few notes of a new song pierce your chest and fill your whole body with absolute bliss—than acknowledgment that your mix tape was not only received and played but enjoyed. It's a dance of sorts, balancing songs you think the listener will love while trying to say everything that otherwise dries up in your throat before you can get out the words.
explicacao: Citação de Libby Cudmore sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “There isn't a better feeling in the world—not an orgasm, not a first kiss, not even that glorious…” articula reflexao, mix-tapes, music em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como music.
